name: Los Calpes Trail (Enduroland MTB Route 12)
name_original: Morrón de Olba y Campos - Los Calpes — circular desde Fuentes de Arenoso (Ruta 12 EndurolandMTB)
description: This difficult 23.2-mile (37.3 km) mountain biking loop from Fuentes de Arenoso offers technical terrain and magnificent views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Puebla De Arenoso, nestled in the Alto Mijares region of Castellón, Spain, offer varied landscapes for riders. The area features mountainous and forested terrain, with challenging climbs and exciting descents. Riders can expect technical sections, including gravel tracks and root-filled paths, alongside scenic routes around the Embalse de Arenoso reservoir and along the Mijares River. This diverse environment provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Puebla De Arenoso The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Calpes Trail (Enduroland MTB Route 12), a difficult 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and technical sections. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Enduroland MTB – Olba Village loop from Puebla de Arenoso, a moderate 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. It offers a mix of climbs and descents through forested areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Enduroland MTB loop from Puebla de Arenoso, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Puebla De Arenoso is defined by mountainous landscapes, dense forests, and the scenic Embalse de Arenoso reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Puebla De Arenoso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De Arenoso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect in Puebla de Arenoso?
A: Puebla de Arenoso offers a diverse range of mountain biking terrain. You'll find challenging climbs and exciting descents through mountainous and forested landscapes. Riders can anticipate technical sections, including gravel tracks with jumps and root-filled paths, especially within the extensive Enduroland MTB project network.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Puebla de Arenoso?
A: While the area is known for its varied terrain, the komoot guide currently features 9 moderate and 6 difficult mountain bike routes. There are no routes specifically graded as easy in this selection, but the broader Enduroland MTB project aims to cater to all skill levels. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enduroland MTB loop from Puebla de Arenoso&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate challenge over 11.1 km.

Q: What are the most scenic mountain bike trails around Puebla de Arenoso?
A: Many routes in Puebla de Arenoso offer stunning views, particularly those around the Embalse de Arenoso reservoir and along the Mijares River. The region's mountainous and forested surroundings provide picturesque scenery. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31705578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enduroland MTB – Olba Village loop from Puebla de Arenoso&lt;/a&gt; takes you through forested areas with a mix of climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33873170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba Village – Enduroland MTB loop from Puebla de Arenoso&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 61.1 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996618165/los-calpes-trail-enduroland-mtb-route-12" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calpes Trail (Enduroland MTB Route 12)&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 37.3 km.

Q: What natural features can I see while mountain biking in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to ride alongside the picturesque Embalse de Arenoso reservoir, with its crystal-clear waters, and the Mijares River, which carves through impressive canyons. Keep an eye out for natural pools like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños - Montanejos Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy the lush forests of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. Puebla de Arenoso boasts a rich historical heritage. You can spot the 12th-century Arab rock castle, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4378255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Arenós&lt;/a&gt;, which overlooks the Embalse de Arenoso. The medieval old town itself is charming, and you might encounter various hermitages as part of a local 'hermitage route'. The area is also part of the historic Camino del Cid.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Puebla de Arenoso?
A: The mountain bike trails in Puebla de Arenoso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Embalse de Arenoso reservoir, and the well-maintained network of routes that offer options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in Puebla de Arenoso?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Puebla de Arenoso are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31705578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enduroland MTB – Olba Village loop from Puebla de Arenoso&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enduroland MTB loop from Puebla de Arenoso&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices among local riders.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Puebla de Arenoso?
A: The region's climate generally allows for mountain biking for much of the year. However, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific facilities or services for mountain bikers in the area?
A: Puebla de Arenoso is part of the Centro BTT Alto Mijares, which serves as a hub for cyclists, providing numerous trails and advice. The broader Enduroland MTB project also indicates a dedicated infrastructure for mountain biking, including specialized parks for skill development.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails?
A: Puebla de Arenoso, with its charming medieval old town, offers local amenities where you can find places to eat and potentially accommodation. The town's unique triangular plaza and historic buildings provide a pleasant setting for post-ride refreshments.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are featured in this guide for Puebla de Arenoso?
A: This guide features 15 distinct mountain bike trails around Puebla de Arenoso, offering a range of experiences from moderate loops to more challenging, longer routes.
tips: An amazing mirador of the Mijares River Valley and the Peñagolosa Massif.

Located in the province of Castellón, we will be surprised by the impressive views that we will have from the Cruz del Viso Viewpoint to Puebla de Arenoso and the reservoir of the same name. It is part of the route of the Cantón-Viso viewpoints of the EnduroldandMTB center.
From its top at 967 meters above sea level, you will have amazing views of the entire Montanejos valley and Alto Mijares. Incredible panoramic views of the Arenoso Reservoir and the Muela de Mijares.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Morrón de Olba y Campos - Los Calpes mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Fuentes de Arenoso. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as the main access point for this EndurolandMTB trail.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a difficult mountain bike tour, designed for experienced riders. It features diverse and challenging terrain, including technical ascents with sections requiring bike portage, and long descents that demand good technical skills and endurance.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a highly varied terrain, ranging from wide tracks to technical singletrack. You'll encounter demanding climbs, including sections where you may need to push your bike, fun zigzagging descents, and some rocky flat sections. The trail also passes through areas that have recovered from a fire.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Alto Mijares region, where this trail is located, generally offers pleasant conditions in spring and autumn. Summers can be very hot, making long rides challenging, while winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Morrón de Olba y Campos - Los Calpes trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on this specific trail, it is a demanding mountain bike route with technical sections and significant distances. If you choose to bring a dog, ensure it is well-conditioned for such a strenuous activity and always keep it under control, preferably on a leash, to ensure the safety of both your pet and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to ride this EndurolandMTB route?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access mountain biking trails in the Alto Mijares region, including this EndurolandMTB route. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in spectacular views. You'll be treated to a View of the Mijares River Valley and Peñagolosa Massif from Morrón de Olba, a View of Puebla de Arenoso and Embalse de Arenoso from Mirador Cruz del Viso, and a View of Embalse de Arenoso and Muela de Mijares from El Morrón de Campos. The trail also passes through the remains of the uninhabited village of El Chorrico.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes or official paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates with several other paths. It covers significant sections of official trails such as La Conquista de Valencia, Camino del Cid (La Conquista de Valencia E06), Pista Morrón de Campos, Pista Fuente San Miguel, Sendero El Chorrico, and Senda Calpes - Morrón, among others. The EndurolandMTB network is designed for connectivity, allowing for extended rides.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources or amenities along the trail?
faqs.answer: The EndurolandMTB GPX tracks often include waypoints for water sources and crucial turns. However, given the length and difficulty of this route, it's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and supplies for the entire duration, as reliable amenities may be scarce on the trail itself.

faqs.question: What makes this EndurolandMTB Route 12 unique?
faqs.answer: This route is notable as the longest in the EndurolandMTB network to date, offering a very complete and diverse mountain biking experience. A unique aspect is the inclusion of 'porteos de bici' (bike portages) during technical climbs, adding to its challenging character. It also leads through the historical remains of the uninhabited village of El Chorrico.
